caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied 4-digit 5418 workforce omits much installation, print, maintenance, permitting and lease work while overstating agency/PR roles.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Programmatic transaction growth is not counted as a V input; it is addressed in pi_dist and terminal discussion.
inputs.pi_dist: No reopened source reports the platform take rate or AI savings pass-through for independent 541850 operators.
inputs.pi_moat: Moat is asset-specific: short leases, non-transferable permits and poor locations can sharply reduce it.
inputs.t50_years: Neither source is a 541850 workflow-adoption survey.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: BTOS is all-business evidence and Place Exchange is a selected platform, not the full code.
inputs.historical_analogs: The source covers one platform’s patterns and must not be read as all-industry adoption.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: S4 discusses plant valuation and owner-operated sales expense, not a representative owner-age survey.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Not documented is not evidence of no target-level succession need.
inputs.active_consolidators: Count is deliberately limited to an active acquirer documented in a reopened primary filing, not a census of all buyers.
inputs.buy_mult: S4 and S7 report billboard cash flow, not EBITDA; accounting definitions, lease treatment and sales-cost normalization can make the proxy materially wrong.
inputs.exit_mult: The source multiple is billboard cash flow, not EBITDA. Exit premium is not used in capture or terminal scoring.
inputs.pricing_structure: This describes billboard plants; 541850 also includes indoor, transit and display-design activities.

Census S1 opens as the NAICS search application without a rendered 541850 result, so the exact industry-scope text is not directly visible at the cited URL; S1 is not score-driving and the packet separately discloses the code's heterogeneous boundary.

The 24% of DOOH spend in 2024 figure in narrative.value_capture is not present on S5's exact page; S5 does support the cited 51% Place Exchange venue mix and platform-specific momentum. Because pi_dist is explicitly an estimate with a platform/pass-through caveat and does not depend on the 24% figure, this does not change C or the thesis.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $382,092 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541850); class <5: 1,543 firms, avg revenue/firm $457,235 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $71,557 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 283 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,808,318 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $283,002 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 130 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,468,000 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $542,742 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 84 firms, avg revenue/firm $12,111,917 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,895,515 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 28 firms, avg revenue/firm $53,150,286 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $8,318,020 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 15 firms, avg revenue/firm $268,195,267 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $41,972,559 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 112

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: AI can automate sales-support, creative, scheduling, reporting and administration, but not the physical operating and site-control work that a true display operator carries. The supplied agency-weighted proxy requires a low-confidence reconstruction.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541850 ($2,510,459,678) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541850 ($8,196,645,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

Census S1 opens as the NAICS search application without a rendered 541850 result, so the exact industry-scope text is not directly visible at the cited URL; S1 is not score-driving and the packet separately discloses the code's heterogeneous boundary.

The 24% of DOOH spend in 2024 figure in narrative.value_capture is not present on S5's exact page; S5 does support the cited 51% Place Exchange venue mix and platform-specific momentum. Because pi_dist is explicitly an estimate with a platform/pass-through caveat and does not depend on the 24% figure, this does not change C or the thesis.
source_review.summary: Mechanics, identity, digests, deterministic dataset injection, fresh finalization, memo rendering and arithmetic all pass. The physical-inventory moat, active-acquirer evidence and OOH-demand evidence support the useful durable-but-asset-specific thesis. The cash-flow-multiple and workforce proxies are prominently disclosed, and two non-material citation issues require publication caveats; neither demonstrates a likely factor, score or thesis reversal.

sources.title: North American Industry Classification System — 541850 Outdoor Advertising
sources.what_it_supports: Official scope: display campaign creation and/or display placement on indoor or outdoor billboards, panels, transit, malls and other structures.

sources.title: Out of Home Advertising Revenue Reaches Record $9.46 Billion
sources.what_it_supports: 2025 U.S. OOH revenue, year-over-year growth, 19 consecutive quarters of growth, and DOOH revenue share and growth.

sources.title: Large Firms With at Least 20 Employees Biggest AI Users
sources.what_it_supports: BTOS broad-business AI use of 17–20% from December 2025 to May 2026 and lower reported use among the smallest firms.

sources.title: What’s Billboard Cashflow?
sources.what_it_supports: Billboard cash-flow definition, direct cost components, and 10–12x sale-multiple guidance for a well-maintained plant with a reasonable lease portfolio.

sources.title: Programmatic OOH Shows Continued Momentum in H1 2025
sources.what_it_supports: Programmatic OOH transaction trends, including outdoor’s share of Place Exchange platform spend, screen growth and deal mix; platform-specific rather than industry-wide.

sources.title: Lamar Advertising Company 2025 Form 10-K
sources.what_it_supports: Lamar’s 2025 outdoor-asset acquisitions, Verde Outdoor acquisition, and purchase-price allocation to site locations, structures, contracts and leases.

sources.title: Does a US Asset Sale Make Sense for Clear Channel?
sources.what_it_supports: Trade-source analysis of 12.5–14x billboard-cash-flow valuation scenarios and an historic Lamar asset-sale reference; not an EBITDA multiple.

title: Indoor and Outdoor Display Advertising
